artifacts.summary: Semantics architecture analysis: W-D distinction, WorldHistory structure, CanonicalQuot path for dense completeness

next_steps: Run /plan 988 to create implementation plan for CanonicalQuot construction

key_findings: Semantics has TWO distinct components: W (world states) and D (durations)
WorldHistory is the fundamental object: function h: D -> W respecting task_rel
Algebraic approach treats D as parameter (imported Rat), violating pure-syntax constraint
Non-algebraic approach hardcodes D=Int, also not from syntax
CanonicalMCS has all witnesses (forward_F, backward_P proven)
TimelineQuot has order properties but not witnesses
Solution: Quotient CanonicalMCS to get CanonicalQuot with BOTH properties

roadmap_reflection.pitfalls_identified: D must not be imported; must emerge from syntax via Cantor
